repositories.brief_summary: Week 18 brought Seq performance improvements, man page overhaul, OCamlFDO compatibility fixes, and active typechecker work.
repositories.new_features_summary: Stdlib Seq functions gained constant-space implementations for repeat, forever, and cycle through recursive closure reuse.
repositories.new_features:
- [#14781](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14781) by [@Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) reduced closure allocation in `Seq.(cycle, repeat, forever)` by reusing tail closures rather than allocating fresh ones on each recursive call, merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- [#14777](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14777) by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) improved `Value_rec_check` by switching to `Ident.Map` and using `Map.union` for the join operation, making recursive-binding checks more efficient, merged by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- [#14778](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14778) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) restored the hard-wired `caml_hot__code_begin` and `caml_hot__code_end` symbol names required by OCamlFDO, and moved `caml_hot` code segment registration into the runtime startup, merged by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
repositories.activity_summary: Six PRs were merged during the week covering man page improvements, Seq performance, OCamlFDO compatibility, typechecker debuggability, and CI security fixes.
repositories.activity:
- [#14786](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14786) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) proposes updating GitHub Actions dependencies to avoid warnings about outdated Node versions in CI runs; open and awaiting review.
- [#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784) by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) proposes making `Seq.cycle` create a true cyclic sequence using a single shared tail closure, reducing per-element memory from 41 words to 1 word; open and under discussion.
- [#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783) by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) makes `Seq.repeat` constant-space by structuring it as a single cyclic closure-and-cons-cell pair; merged after the week boundary.
- [#14782](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14782) by [@shym](https://github.com/shym) overhauled `ocamlc`'s man page with improved formatting, style, synopsis reformatting, sub-list rendering, and `-o` option clarifications, plus minor fixes to `ocamlopt`'s man page; merged 2026-05-03.
- [#14781](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14781) by [@Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) reduced closure allocation in `Seq.cycle`, `Seq.repeat`, and `Seq.forever`; merged 2026-05-01.
- [#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14779) by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) proposes fixing the typechecker loop reported in [#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769) by detecting cycles in `update_level`; open and under review.
- [#14778](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14778) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) fixed OCamlFDO compatibility by restoring hard-wired symbol names and registering the hot code segment in `startup_nat.c`; merged 2026-04-29.
- [#14777](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14777) by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) made a small cleanup to `value_rec_check.ml`; merged 2026-04-29.
- [#14776](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14776) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) fixes a missing `wrap_trace_gadts_instance` call in `moregen` when checking first-class module inclusion; open and under review.
- [#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) proposes extensible IO channels implementing the RFC for modular IO, introducing a new channel abstraction alongside the existing C channels; open and under review.
- [#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) documents a newly found typechecker loop triggered by heavy use of conjunctive types after `Keep_expansion`; merged 2026-04-29.
- [#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676) by [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128) fixed shell injection vulnerabilities in GitHub Actions workflows; merged 2026-05-04.
- [#14772](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14772) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) improved typechecker debuggability by adding a printer for unscoped identifiers used in `Rawprinttyp` and `Gprinttyp`; merged 2026-04-27.
- [#14771](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14771) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) added a breakpoint capability on `tfunctor` nodes in the typechecker debugger; merged 2026-04-27.
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussions centred on constant-space Seq sequences and a typechecker loop affecting conjunctive types with expansion constraints.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784) and [#14785](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14785) sparked a debate between [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) and [@Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) on whether `Seq.cycle` should produce a truly cyclic (sharing) sequence or a constant-space infinite one; [#14785](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14785) was closed as superseded by [#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784).
- [#14780](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14780) opened by [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) reports that `module type of M` when `M` is accessed through an alias strengthens functor parameter signatures unexpectedly, blocking application to sibling modules; this attracted discussion about the intended semantics of module alias strengthening.
- [#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14779) discusses the root cause of the loop in [#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769): `update_level` encountering the expanded type of its own argument, and the correct fix for detecting and breaking that cycle.
repositories.trends_summary: The week shows ongoing investment in Stdlib Seq space efficiency, typechecker correctness and debuggability, and native-code tooling compatibility.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Seq constant-space work** is converging: [#14781](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14781) landed, [#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783) is merged post-week, and [#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784) proposes a fully cyclic `Seq.cycle`, showing sustained effort from [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) and [@Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) to minimise allocation in lazy sequences.
- **Typechecker debuggability** improvements by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([#14771](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14771), [#14772](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14772)) continue a pattern of adding internal tooling to support OCaml 5.5 typechecker development.
- **Native-code tooling compatibility** work ([#14778](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14778) for OCamlFDO) reflects continued effort to keep the runtime compatible with profile-guided optimisation workflows.
- **Extensible IO channels** proposed in [#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) marks a significant RFC-driven feature attempt for the standard library.
repositories.issues_summary: One new issue was opened on functor parameter strengthening via module aliases; one old cross-compiler build issue was closed.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [#14658](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14658) opened by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) requests that `OCAMLRUNPARAM` accept size suffixes such as `s=2MB`, making memory-size parameters more ergonomic to specify.

repositories.brief_summary: New OpamFilename.Unix module for cross-platform path handling merged, with several API and patch system PRs opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: New OpamFilename.Unix module abstracts over '/' paths on all platforms; dummy file comparison fix in OpamFile.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`OpamFilename.Unix` module** added to `src/core/opamFilename.ml` and `src/core/opamFilename.mli`, providing path manipulation that always uses `/` as the separator regardless of the current system (Windows or Unix). Includes `Unix.Dir`, `Unix.Base`, and `Unix.Op` submodules, plus `of_filename`/`to_filename` conversions. The new `unix_filename` and `unix_dirname` types are also exported from `opamTypes.mli` ([#6914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6914))
- Fixed **`OpamFile` dummy file comparison** to detect dummy filenames even when they have a directory prefix, by comparing basenames rather than exact equality. This resolves a subtle bug triggered when working with relative paths in the repository loading code ([#6913](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6913))
- **GHA depexts CI improvements**: the GitHub Actions depexts workflow now always uses the latest 'stable' version of each Linux distribution and installs the already-present OCaml package via `ocaml-system`, improving CI reproducibility ([#6905](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6905))
repositories.activity_summary: Seven new PRs opened covering patch handling, path modules, benchmarking, and documentation; several long-running API refactors remain active.
repositories.activity:
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened **patch system overhaul** PR [#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915) (1777 additions) to allow `dir -> file` type changes when applying patches, a previously unsupported scenario that caused failures
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened **path element name modules** PR [#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917) (2072 additions across 41 files), a large refactor adding explicit modules for well-known opam path component names to improve consistency
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened **`opam install` benchmark** PR [#6912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6912), adding infrastructure to measure install performance, used to validate the build-directory-cleanup change in [#6906](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6906)
- [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) opened [#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921) to **homogenise manpage reference highlighting**, addressing the long-standing issue [#6112](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6112) — their first contribution, made as a student at Université Paris-Cité
- [Corentin Coulombel](https://github.com/ccoulombel) opened [#6920](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6920) to **improve visibility of the depopts filter note** in documentation, addressing [#5367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5367)
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)'s **build directory cleanup** PR [#6906](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6906) (queued, labeled `AREA: PERFORMANCE`) benchmarked at 0.3% degradation for regular installs but ~5% improvement on IO-constrained filesystems
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb)'s **`chdir` removal** PR [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) is stalled awaiting a decision on whether to keep deprecated API entries given that `opam-rt` also needs patching
- Long-running API refactors remain open: **repository root abstraction** by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) ([#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680), open since September 2025), and **HTTP repository format improvement** using tar.gz directly ([#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625), open since July 2025)
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)'s **repository load-from-diff** PR [#6871](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6871) continues to be reviewed
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened two API design issues: [#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919) proposing a `prefix` field for `.install` files, and [#6918](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6918) proposing to change `OpamFile.typed_file` to return an option
- [#6896](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6896) (`OpamFilename.might_escape` Windows fix) was closed as the change was integrated into [#6897](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6897)
repositories.discussions_summary: Issue #6911 on --assume-built compiler install revealed upstream docs and opam file needing updates; #6916 traced to MSYS2 CA cert issue.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) reported that **`--assume-built` with `ocaml-variants` fails** in [#6911](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6911); [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reproduced the issue and identified that both the upstream OCaml compiler documentation and `ocaml-variants.opam` need updating — the new `make INSTALL_MODE=opam install` instruction creates `ocaml-compiler-fixup.sh` instead of `ocaml-variants-fixup.sh`, causing the installation to fail. Issue closed pending upstream fixes.
- [#6913](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6913) PR discussion between [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) and [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) clarified how **dummy filenames can acquire directory prefixes** when using relative paths during repository loading work ([#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)), motivating the fix
- [Issue #5367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5367) and [issue #6112](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6112) each attracted **first-time contributors** ([Corentin Coulombel](https://github.com/ccoulombel) and [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) respectively) who opened PRs addressing them this week
repositories.trends_summary: Active effort to improve Windows path handling and cross-platform correctness, alongside growing investment in performance benchmarking.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Windows and cross-platform path handling** continues to be a focus: the new `OpamFilename.Unix` module ([#6914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6914)), the closed `might_escape` Windows fix ([#6896](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6896)), and the large path element name modules PR ([#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917)) all address correctness on non-Unix systems
- **Performance measurement infrastructure** is being put in place: [#6912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6912) adds `opam install` benchmarks, enabling data-driven decisions like the IO-filesystem trade-off analysis in [#6906](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6906)
- **API surface cleanup** is an ongoing theme with several concurrent efforts: removing `chdir` dependencies ([#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)), abstracting repository roots ([#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680)), and proposing `typed_file` option changes ([#6918](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6918))
- **New contributors engaging with documentation issues**: both [#6112](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6112) and [#5367](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5367) are receiving first-time contributor PRs this week, suggesting these labelled issues are effective at attracting participation

repositories.brief_summary: Community discussion on neovim inlay hints and code lens configuration; automated Nix flake lock update.
repositories.activity_summary: Automated flake.lock update PR remained open with refreshed Nix dependencies including merlin and nixpkgs.
repositories.activity: - Automated **flake.lock update** ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)) refreshed Nix dependencies: `merlin5_3` bumped to commit `03c8e3f` (2026-04-30) and `nixpkgs` overlays updated to `bb13393` (2026-05-01), keeping the Nix development environment current.
repositories.discussions_summary: User [curbe454](https://github.com/curbe454) shared a working LazyVim configuration for inlay hints and code lens in neovim on issue #1521.
repositories.notable_discussions: - On [#1521](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1521) ("Enable Code Lens / Inlay-Hints in neovim?"), [curbe454](https://github.com/curbe454) posted a working **LazyVim configuration** for `ocamllsp` that enables **inlay hints** and **code lens**. The configuration uses nested `{ enable = true }` objects (e.g. `extendedHover = { enable = true }`) rather than flat boolean values, and requires explicitly calling `vim.lsp.codelens.enable(true)` via an `LspAttach` autocmd. [curbe454](https://github.com/curbe454) noted that inlay hints for `let`-binding type annotations work once code lens is enabled this way, leaving this as a reference for other neovim users hitting the same issue.
repositories.contributors_summary: [curbe454](https://github.com/curbe454) contributed community knowledge by sharing a working neovim configuration on issue #1521.
repositories.contributors: - [@curbe454](https://github.com/curbe454) commented on [#1521](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1521) with a detailed working neovim/LazyVim setup for inlay hints and code lens, including debugging output from `:checkhealth vim.lsp` and `:lua print_table(vim.lsp.codelens.get())`.

repositories.brief_summary: Merlin 5.7.1 released with typer cache fix and Misc module refactoring; ocamlgrep integration proposed.
repositories.new_features_summary: Fixed typer cache invalidation for new .cmi files; refactored Misc module to reduce compiler diff.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed **typer cache invalidation** bug where a new `.cmi` file appearing in the build path (e.g. when dune builds a new module) would not trigger cache invalidation, causing stale type information. The fix improves `persistent_env.ml` to track missing persistent structs regardless of the `allow_hidden` flag, addressing a root cause identified by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) in [ocaml/ocaml#12246](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12246) two years ago ([#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062))
- Refactored the **`Misc` module** from `merlin_utils` (`src/utils/misc.ml`) to `ocaml_utils` (`src/ocaml/utils/misc.ml`) to reduce the diff against the upstream compiler, making future synchronization easier. Merlin-specific functions (`time_spent`, `unitname`) were moved to `src/utils/std.ml`, and a duplicate `modules_in_path` function was removed ([#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059))
- Released **Merlin v5.7.1-504** incorporating the cache fix and module refactor, with release notes noting this is intended as the final OCaml 5.4 release ([v5.7.1-504](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/releases/tag/v5.7.1-504))
repositories.activity_summary: Dependabot opened a Nix CI action bump; ocamlgrep integration was proposed as a new project-wide type-aware search feature.

repositories.discussions_summary: Martin Jambon proposed integrating LexiFi's ocamlgrep type-aware search tool into Merlin, with LexiFi backing.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened issue [#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063) proposing integration of **ocamlgrep** (formerly `grep_cmt`) into Merlin. The tool enables project-wide type-aware pattern search (e.g. `(__ : int list)` to find all expressions of type `int list`) and would benefit from Merlin's `.cmt` file discovery and cross-version compiler-libs management
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) noted the proposal is interesting and asked whether [Sherlodoc](https://github.com/tarides/ocaml-eglot#search-for-values) already covers similar ground, observing that `ocamlgrep` has unique advantages for searching beyond public APIs ([#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063))
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) confirmed **full LexiFi support** for the proposal and that [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) is collaborating with LexiFi to drive it forward ([#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063))
- During review of [#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062), [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) traced the cache bug's root cause to `Env.check_state_consistency` in `persistent_env.ml`, connecting it to a two-year-old prediction by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) in [ocaml/ocaml#12246](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12246); the fix was refined before merging
repositories.trends_summary: Continued effort to reduce Merlin's diff from the upstream OCaml compiler, and growing interest in richer code search tooling.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Ongoing work to **reduce the diff between Merlin and the upstream OCaml compiler** by moving shared modules (like `Misc`) into compiler-synced locations; this reduces maintenance burden for future OCaml version upgrades ([#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059))
- Renewed interest in **project-wide, type-aware code search** for OCaml, with the ocamlgrep proposal ([#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063)) signaling appetite for deeper IDE tooling beyond completions and hover types
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson and Ulysse drove code changes; Martin Jambon, Xavier Van de Woestyne, and Nicolás Ojeda Bär contributed discussions.
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) authored the typer cache invalidation fix ([#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062)) and the `misc.ml` synchronization refactor ([#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)), crediting [@JimmyZJX](https://github.com/JimmyZJX) for identifying the cache bug and providing a reproducible test case
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed both PRs, identified the deeper root cause in `persistent_env.ml` for the cache bug, pushed a refined alternative fix, added the changelog entry, and released Merlin v5.7.1-504 ([#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062), [#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059), [v5.7.1-504](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/releases/tag/v5.7.1-504))
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened the ocamlgrep integration proposal and is coordinating with LexiFi to drive the work forward ([#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063))
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) participated in the ocamlgrep discussion, contextualizing it against existing Sherlodoc search capabilities ([#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063))
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) confirmed LexiFi's backing for the ocamlgrep proposal ([#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063))

repositories.brief_summary: 3.23.0 alpha releases published; new %{pkg:foo:lib:file} pforms, ppx_optcomp bootstrap support, and single-context compilation work.
repositories.new_features_summary: New %{pkg:foo:lib:file} package install pforms, ppx_optcomp bootstrap support, and source-files-changed watch event.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`%{pkg:foo:lib:file}` pforms** for querying package install layouts landed in [#14200](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14200) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), closing the long-standing issue [#14193](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14193) and ancient [#3378](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3378); a companion draft [#14373](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14373) extends this to install layouts for full package sets.
- Support for **a subset of `ppx_optcomp` conditionals in the bootstrap build** added by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) in [#14404](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14404), with follow-on clean-ups to the PPX lexer and conditional streaming ([#14406](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14406), [#14408](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14408)).
- **`%test_module` blocks are now stripped during bootstrap**, preventing spurious build failures and reducing bootstrap surface area ([#14355](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14355) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)).
- **Source-files-changed event** added to dune's watch-mode RPC stream by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([#14396](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14396)), enabling clients to react to file-system changes without polling.
- Dune's **trace output now includes self rusage snapshots** (CPU, memory, I/O counters) at the start and end of builds via [#14402](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14402) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- **`Compilation_context.t` is now built per `Compilation_mode.t`** — the large single-context architectural refactor landed in [#13346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13346) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), followed by [#14410](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14410) (`Compilation_mode.to_list`) and [#14411](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14411) (ocaml-index emission per compilation context), bringing per-mode separate object directories and cleaner Melange/native mode separation.
- **Fixed stale staging paths in `dune promote`** that could cause promotion to fail when a target changes from a directory to a file ([#14371](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14371) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)).
- **Fixed dialect ppx correction targets** being incorrectly shared with ppx inputs in [#14348](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14348) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro).
- **`dune-site` no longer emits spurious entering/leaving directory log lines** — a one-line fix in [#14356](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14356) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w).
- **dune-rpc connection error handling hardened**: write-side disconnects are now treated consistently ([#14384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14384)), pending requests are terminated on disconnect ([#14368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14368)), and write errors now raise `Connection_dead` ([#14361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14361)) — all by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- **Relocatable compiler integration draft** opened by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) as [#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357), building on [#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229) to use the relocatable compiler in package builds.
repositories.activity_summary: Release 3.23.0 alpha cycle is active; broad test coverage expansion, RPC refactoring, and several large feature drafts opened.
repositories.activity:
- **Three 3.23.0 alpha pre-releases** (`alpha0`, `alpha1`, `alpha2`) were published to opam-repository on 2026-04-30 by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), with the release tracking issue at [#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211) and the release PR [#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212) still open.
- **Newly opened feature drafts** represent substantial upcoming work: **allocation profiling** support ([#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)), **action runners** revival ([#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)), **`include_qualified` directory renaming** ([#13691](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13691) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)), and **`-I` flag filtering per-module** to match dependency filtering ([#14186](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14186) by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb)).
- **RPC subsystem refactoring** merged across multiple PRs: server registry moved into its own module ([#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14367)), Dune_engine.Rpc moved to Rpc.Global ([#14362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14362)), read/write paths simplified ([#14366](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14366)), and `Mutex.protect` refactored ([#14399](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14399)) — all by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg). A debug dump of active RPC requests is in draft at [#14405](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14405).
- **Extensive observational test baselines** added by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) to lock in current behavior before upcoming changes: per-module include flags ([#14375](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14375)), unreferenced transitive module ([#14374](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14374)), per-module rebuild counts ([#14310](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14310)), broad-include leak of wrapped-library internal names ([#14317](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14317)), unreferenced transitive library dep ([#14332](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14332)), sibling-module and single-module consumer unreferenced-dep guards ([#14309](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14309), [#14308](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14308)), cross-lib reads of unwrapped libs with `private_modules` ([#14388](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14388)), cross-lib reads of action-preprocessed deps ([#14383](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14383)), single-module leaf libs ([#14389](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14389)), and menhir grammars referencing library deps ([#14391](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14391)).
- **`ocamldep` refactoring** by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) eliminated named targets ([#14385](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14385)) and simplified sandbox handling ([#14386](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14386)), cleaning up the dep-rules interface.
- **Package management work**: [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) continued pkg work with a test for applying patches to packages without sources ([#14353](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14353)) and directory-to-file target change promotion test ([#14370](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14370)); [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) simplified `Pkg_rules.source_files` ([#14301](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14301)) and has [#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13792) open for resolving directory symlinks in fetched targets.
- **OxCaml / trunk build** fixes from [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247): trunk build fix ([#14319](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14319)) and crash reproduction for [#14272](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14272) in [#14289](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14289).
- **`DUNE_PKG` environment variable PR** ([#14318](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14318) by [@desumn](https://github.com/desumn)) was closed after the dev-meeting concluded there is insufficient user demand for the feature at this time.
- **Remove explicit OCaml transitive dependency files** PR ([#14407](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14407) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)) is open, aiming to simplify the transitive dependency tracking mechanism.
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) fixed the `--note-remote` logic in dune-release tooling ([#14111](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14111)) and added dune-release target configuration variables ([#14378](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14378)).
- A test reproducing the `dune build @doc` failure when odoc is installed via `dune tools install` was added by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) in [#14234](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14234).
repositories.discussions_summary: Dev-meeting rejected DUNE_PKG env var; 3.23.0 release tracker active with alpha pre-releases published.
repositories.notable_discussions:

repositories.trends_summary: Architectural consolidation around per-compilation-mode contexts, RPC hardening, and package install layout expansion are the dominant themes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **per-compilation-mode context separation** (native vs. Melange vs. other modes each getting their own `Compilation_context.t`) is a multi-week architectural direction, with [#13346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13346) landing this week and follow-on PRs [#14411](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14411) and [#14410](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14410) extending it. This unlocks per-mode object directories and cleaner mode-specific rules.
- **Package install layout as a first-class dune concept** is expanding rapidly: `%{pkg:foo:lib:file}` pforms ([#14200](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14200)), install layouts for package sets ([#14373](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14373)), and integration with the relocatable compiler ([#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357)) all point toward dune taking fuller ownership of installed-package layouts.
- **RPC subsystem hardening** continues as a sustained effort: multiple disconnect and error handling fixes this week ([#14361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14361), [#14368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14368), [#14384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14384)) combined with structural refactors ([#14362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14362), [#14366](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14366), [#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14367)) suggest the RPC layer is being prepared for more demanding use cases, including the planned action runners ([#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240)).
- **Observational test baselines** by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) (10+ PRs this week) follow a pattern of locking in current behavior before planned changes to dependency filtering and include-flag handling, indicating larger module-level dependency changes are coming.

repositories.brief_summary: odoc 3.2.0 released; OxCaml compatibility merged; zero-alloc annotation support in progress.
repositories.new_features_summary: OxCaml AST compatibility landed; fix for Arrow_functor type expression tracking merged for 3.2.0.
repositories.new_features:
- **OxCaml compiler-libs compatibility** merged by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), updating the loader to handle breaking AST changes in the latest OxCaml release including new functor parameter modes, `Tmod_constraint` variant, `Tsig_jkind`/`Tstr_jkind` declarations, and representation type annotations (`Trepr`, `Ttyp_repr`) — all new OxCaml nodes are gracefully ignored to keep odoc compilable ([#1423](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1423))
- **`@@zero_alloc` annotation support** for OxCaml signatures in progress by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV), parsing `[@@zero_alloc]`, `[@@zero_alloc strict]`, and `[@@zero_alloc opt]` variants from the source and retaining them in parsed output ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422))
- **Fix for `Not_found` crash** when building docs for packages using OCaml 5.5's `Arrow_functor` type expression, caused by missing identifier tracking in `lang_of.ml` — merged by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) ([#1427](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1427), closes [#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426))

repositories.activity:
- **New PRs opened this week**: [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) opened two OxCaml-related PRs — `@@zero_alloc` annotation support ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) and an OxCaml implementation file test to demonstrate rendering of OxCaml code without an interface file ([#1424](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1424))
- **odoc 3.2.0 released** by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) via release preparation PR ([#1425](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1425)), tagging [3.2.0](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/releases/tag/3.2.0) which includes persistent LaTeX macros, multi-file `markdown-generate`, OxCaml support, OCaml 5.5 support, and several bug fixes
- **OxCaml AST compatibility** merged ([#1423](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1423)) fixing odoc's compilation against the latest OxCaml compiler-libs, with dependencies on [oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39) and [oxcaml/opam-repository#42](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/42)
- **OCaml 5.5 `base` package crash** reported by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) ([#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426)) and immediately fixed the same day via [#1427](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1427)
- **`@@zero_alloc` annotation** issue tracked by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([#1418](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1418)) is now being addressed by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) in [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on zero_alloc PR clarified handling of strict/opt variants and formatting details.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) reviewed [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422) and raised questions about whether `@@zero_alloc opt` is user-relevant enough to display, leading [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) to investigate OxCaml's toplevel behavior and confirm that `strict` and `opt` variants should be preserved in the rendered output while `assume` maps to a plain `@@zero_alloc`
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) also flagged a potential formatting issue with `O.cut` placement that could affect output for existing docs ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422))
repositories.trends_summary: Growing OxCaml integration effort with multiple contributors addressing annotations, AST compatibility, and test coverage.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml compatibility** is becoming an active development focus, with three separate PRs this week ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), [#1423](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1423), [#1424](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1424)) tackling different aspects: compiler-libs AST changes, annotation rendering, and test infrastructure for OxCaml-specific constructs
- **Rapid issue turnaround** seen with [#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426) being reported and fixed ([#1427](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1427)) on the same day, reflecting responsive maintenance around the 3.2.0 release
repositories.contributors_summary: Jon Ludlam, art-w, and Marek Kubica drove the week's work across releases, OxCaml compatibility, and new features.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) prepared and published the 3.2.0 release ([#1425](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1425)), reported the OCaml 5.5 `base` documentation crash ([#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426)), and merged the immediate fix ([#1427](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1427))
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) authored the OxCaml compiler-libs compatibility PR ([#1423](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1423)) and provided detailed review feedback on the zero_alloc annotation PR ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)), including discussion of variant handling and output formatting

repositories.brief_summary: Tutorial accuracy fix, new community event listing, and expanded OCaml Planet content with 14 new posts scraped.
repositories.new_features_summary: Corrected O(1) complexity description in Hash Tables tutorial from 'linear' to 'constant' time.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed a factual error in the **Hash Tables tutorial** where O(1) complexity was incorrectly described as "linear" instead of "constant" time complexity, contributed by [@LordPatate](https://github.com/LordPatate) ([#3630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3630))
- **OCaml Planet** received 14 new blog post entries across five batches of automated scraping, covering topics including OCaml CI, OxCaml, QCheck generators, Odoc notebooks, and community weeknotes ([#3628](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3628), [#3629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3629), [#3632](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3632), [#3634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3634), [#3635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3635))
repositories.activity_summary: Two community PRs opened: adding a recurring London OCaml event and expanding Hashtbl vs Map tutorial documentation.
repositories.activity:
- **New PR opened**: [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho) proposed adding the recurring **Caml in the Capital** event to the community listings, and correcting the S-REPLS location from "London" to "England" (since it moves around South England, including Cambridge) ([#3633](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3633))
- **New PR opened**: [@LordPatate](https://github.com/LordPatate) opened a follow-up to their complexity fix, expanding the **Hashtbl vs Map tutorial** to better explain when to choose one over the other — noting that the existing doc only highlighted Hashtbl's advantages without explaining Maps' functional interface and ordering benefits ([#3631](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3631)), with review feedback from [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac)

repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on PR #3631 around capitalization conventions and phrasing for the Hashtbl/Map tutorial expansion.

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with 23 merges including new packages like lwd 0.5, notty-miou 0.0.1, wol 1.0, and the oxcaml virtual package.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages include lwd 0.5 reactive docs, notty-miou 0.0.1, Wake-on-LAN libraries, cattery pool for Miou, and oxcaml virtual package.
repositories.new_features:
- **oxcaml virtual package** added to opam-repository by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js), providing a signalling mechanism for OxCaml dependency with a helpful error message guiding users to set up the OxCaml opam remote correctly ([#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809))
- **lwd 0.5** released as 7 packages by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos): includes `quick_sample`/`quick_release` API additions, detection and reporting of nested sampling, fixed mutation handling in Join nodes, updated `brr-lwd` for multiple reactive attributes/events, and migrated `nottui` to notty-community ([#29832](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29832))
- **notty-miou 0.0.1** and **nottui-miou 0.0.1** — first release of terminal library bindings for the Miou scheduler by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) ([#29833](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29833))
- **wol 1.0**, **wol-eio 1.0**, and **wol-mirage 1.0** — new Wake-on-LAN magic packet construction packages with Eio and MirageOS variants by [Ryan Gibb](https://github.com/RyanGibb) ([#29831](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29831))
- **cattery 0.0.1** — first release of a pool library for the Miou scheduler by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) ([#29810](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29810))
- **ciao_lwt 0.2** — compatibility with OCaml 5.4 and 5.5, plus improved handling of bound expressions that are not function applications in Lwt migration by [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) ([#29811](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29811))
- **merlin 5.7.1-504** — fixes a cache invalidation issue introduced by hidden deps in OCaml 5.2, and improves merged index format to produce smaller index files with cross-references by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([#29828](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29828))
- **hdr_histogram 0.0.5** — ~1.8x faster value recording by not releasing the runtime lock, new `Hdr_histogram.record_value` using `int` instead of `int64`, new `hdr_percentiles_print` binding, and Windows support by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([#29817](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29817))
- **ppx_deriving_jsonschema 0.0.7** — adds `@@jsonschema.compact_variants`, `@@jsonschema.format`, `@@jsonschema.description`, `~ocaml_doc` flag, `@jsonschema.attrs` composite attribute, `@jsonschema.default` for record fields, and makes option types nullable; fixes schema generation for parametric recursive types ([#29819](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29819))
- **charset 0.3.0** — new release by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) ([#29839](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29839))
- **conf-git-daemon** — new conf package added ([cb8d110](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/commit/cb8d11035ca0fe29e1b7cc04544ad24ba7bcad50))
- **conf-dwarfutils** extended to support OpenSUSE Leap and Tumbleweed, CentOS 9+, with added `zlib`/`zstd` depexts and restriction to distros shipping `libdwarf.pc` ([#29202](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29202))

repositories.discussions_summary: Discussions around the oxcaml virtual package design, lwd 0.5 dependency constraint fixes, and BER MetaOCaml avoid-version approach.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **oxcaml virtual package** PR ([#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)) prompted discussion about the best approach for providing user-friendly error messages vs `available: false`. [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) both supported the action-required approach with purposeful version string ordering (`action-required < archived < latest`) as more user-friendly than opaque solver failures
- The **lwd 0.5** release ([#29832](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29832)) required two rounds of review to fix dependency constraint issues: `tyxml-lwd` needed a `tyxml >= 4.6.0` lower bound and `lwd` needed a `qcheck >= 0.14` bound, both fixed upstream before merging
- The **BER MetaOCaml avoid-version** PR ([#29815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29815)) is investigating how to prevent OCaml variant compilers from being selected by the solver for unrelated switches; [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) spotted the `flags` vs `tags` field naming issue
- **dune 3.23.0~alpha2** PR ([#29824](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29824)) was preceded by two cancelled alpha releases (alpha0 and alpha1, [#29822](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29822), [#29823](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29823)) due to release process errors by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
repositories.trends_summary: Growing Miou scheduler ecosystem with multiple new packages; continued OCaml 5.5 compatibility work across repository.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **Miou scheduler ecosystem** is growing rapidly with three new packages this week: cattery 0.0.1 (pool), notty-miou 0.0.1 (terminals), nottui-miou 0.0.1 (UI) — all from [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) and the robur-coop community ([#29810](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29810), [#29833](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29833))
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** is an active concern: multiple packages needed bounds or fixes this week including caqti-miou ([#29814](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29814)) and core.v0.17.1 ([#29820](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29820)) due to `Gc.stat` record changes
- **OxCaml integration** into the main opam-repository infrastructure is beginning, with the virtual package PR ([#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)) establishing the pattern for how OxCaml-dependent packages can signal their requirements
- **Windows platform support** continues to expand: conf-mingw-w64-* packages mechanised and cleaned up ([#29795](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29795)), and conf-perl Cygwin/MSYS2 support under review ([#29613](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29613))
- **Dune 3.23.0 alpha** is being prepared with notable breaking changes (user rules sandboxed by default, removal of `--makefile` flag, mandatory manual promotion of opam files), indicating a significant upcoming release

repositories.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5 Wasm support completed, several compiler and runtime bug fixes merged, QuickJS-NG support proposed.
repositories.new_features_summary: Wasm OCaml 5.5 CMI embedding, QuickJS-NG runtime shim proposed, DOM API type corrections opened.
repositories.new_features:
- **Wasm toplevel separate-compilation** now embeds CMIs in the output `.wasmo` zip file (`embedded_files` entry), fixing compatibility with OCaml 5.5's runtime-resolved `Config.standard_library` ([#2220](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2220))
- **Wasm dynlink** gains a stub for `caml_dynlink_parse_ld_conf` (conditionally compiled for OCaml >= 5.5), allowing dynlink-using programs to link against OCaml 5.5+ ([#2220](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2220))
- Fixed **`Int64.of_string` in the Wasm runtime**: the empty-input guard now checks `$i >= $len` (cursor past end of string) rather than only checking for a completely empty string, preventing out-of-bounds traps on inputs like `"+"`, `"-"`, or `"0x"` ([#2223](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2223))
- Fixed **`caml_oo_cache_id` in the JS runtime**: a missing `return` statement in `runtime/js/obj.js` caused the function to return `undefined` instead of the allocated cache slot id ([#2224](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2224))
- Fixed **compiler peephole rewrite for string concatenation**: `Plus` removed from the commutative-operators list in `Js_traverse.simpl`; JavaScript `+` is non-commutative on strings, so the `x = e + x` → `x += e` rewrite silently reversed operands, causing `Filename.temp_file` to return paths of the form `<filename>/<temp_dir>` under whole-program compilation with `use-js-string` ([#2228](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2228))
- Fixed **deadcode elimination**: conditional branches that become identical after unused argument removal no longer break the compiler's code invariant ([#2217](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2217))
- Fixed **`Js_traverse.simpl` optimization ordering**: dead branches of constant-condition `if` statements are now eliminated before visiting, preventing variables declared in dead branches from being incorrectly added to the `declared` set ([#2218](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2218))
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs target QuickJS-NG support, DOM API updates, OCaml 5.6, and older-browser docs; two tracking issues closed.
repositories.activity:
- **QuickJS-NG support** proposed by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) in [#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229): adds a `quickjs` dune profile with `tools/quickjs_wrapper.exe`, a `runtime/js/quickjs_shim.js` compatibility shim filling in Node-only globals, fixes to host-portability bugs surfaced by QuickJS, and a `make tests-quickjs` CI target; stacked on [#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216) and [#2228](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2228)
- **DOM API type corrections** opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) in [#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221) (3215 additions, 91 deletions): fixes `Dom_html.inputElement.defaultChecked` type from `js_string t prop` to `bool t prop`, corrects `Dom_html.createVideo` return type from `audioElement t` to `videoElement t`, and fixes `Dom_html.selection.collapse` signature to match the actual JS `Selection.collapse(node, offset?)` API; addresses [#679](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/679) and [#453](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/453)
- **Initial OCaml 5.6 support** draft opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) in [#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227) (286 additions across 17 files)
- **Oxcaml 5.2.0minus31 update** draft opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) in [#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225), with [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) raising a question about backward compatibility with the previous OxCaml version
- **Browser compatibility documentation** (new `manual/browser-compat.wiki` chapter on downleveling with Babel and core-js) remains open in [#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo); also fixes `ECMASCRIPT.md` (async/await are ES2017, not ES2016) and adds Promise and template literals
- **OCaml 5.5 support tracking issue** [#2193](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2193) closed after [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) confirmed master supports OCaml 5.5; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) noted the 5.5 release candidate is expected by mid-May
- **Wasm testing tracking issue** [#2061](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2061) closed after upstream dependencies were resolved
- **`dune.inc` cleanup** merged by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) in [#2222](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2222), removing 234 lines through rule consolidation
- **OxCaml CI workflow fix** merged by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) in [#2219](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2219)
- Long-running draft PRs remain active: **physical equality hints** ([#1745](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1745)), **OCaml upstream hints consumption** ([#1721](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1721)), **compiler substitution speedup** ([#2185](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2185)), and **OCaml 5.5 full compiler+runtime support** ([#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195), currently conflicting)
- **`Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` bindings** remain open from [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) in [#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070)
repositories.discussions_summary: Feature request for a neutral JS target env for runtimes like QuickJS; discussion on CPS tail-call recursion depth issues.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened [#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226) requesting a `--target-env=neutral` option for JS runtimes other than browsers and Node.js (e.g. QuickJS), noting that using `--target-env=browser` for QuickJS targets is counter-intuitive; this connects thematically to [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)'s [#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229) QuickJS-NG support PR
- In [#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215), [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) identified the root cause of their compatibility issue as `"Uncaught InternalError: too much recursion"` in a CPS-style function after transpilation with Babel, and noted the broader difficulty of debugging CPS tail-call depth on constrained JS engines; [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) asked for Babel config details and the last working js_of_ocaml version
repositories.trends_summary: Active push toward broader JS runtime support (QuickJS), OCaml version compatibility (5.5 completed, 5.6 started), and compiler correctness.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Broader JS runtime compatibility** is a clear focus: the QuickJS-NG PR ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)), the `--target-env=neutral` feature request ([#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226)), and the browser compatibility documentation ([#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)) all push toward running js_of_ocaml output on engines beyond Node.js and mainstream browsers
- **Rapid OCaml version cadence**: OCaml 5.5 support was completed this week ([#2220](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2220), [#2193](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2193)) and OCaml 5.6 work has already begun ([#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227)), reflecting the project's need to track upstream OCaml releases closely
- **Compiler correctness fixes** around edge cases in the JS code generation: three separate optimizer bugs fixed this week ([#2217](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2217), [#2218](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2218), [#2228](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2228)), suggesting ongoing investment in the correctness of the peephole and simplification passes
repositories.contributors_summary: Jérôme Vouillon and @hhugo drove all activity, with Stéphane Glondu opening issues and Kate commenting on OCaml 5.5 timing.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged fixes for deadcode elimination ([#2217](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2217)), `Js_traverse.simpl` ordering ([#2218](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2218)), `caml_oo_cache_id` ([#2224](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2224)), and `Int64.of_string` in the Wasm runtime ([#2223](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2223)), and fixed the OxCaml CI workflow ([#2219](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2219)); also opened draft PRs for Oxcaml 5.2.0minus31 ([#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225)), initial OCaml 5.6 support ([#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227)), and the large DOM API updates ([#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) merged Wasm OCaml 5.5 completion ([#2220](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2220)), `dune.inc` cleanup ([#2222](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2222)), and the `Filename.concat` operand-order bug fix ([#2228](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2228)); opened the QuickJS-NG support PR ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)) and the browser compatibility documentation PR ([#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)); also commented on [#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225) regarding OxCaml backward compatibility, and acknowledged the OCaml 5.5 support issue ([#2193](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2193))
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened feature request [#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226) for a neutral JS target environment and continued discussion in [#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215) identifying CPS tail-call recursion depth as a practical issue after Babel transpilation
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) commented on [#2193](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2193) noting the OCaml 5.5 release candidate timing aligns well with the expected js_of_ocaml release

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with 20 commits across typing, flambda2, backend, DWARF, and metaprogramming, alongside steady 5.4 merge work.
repositories.new_features_summary: New kind abbreviations, `float64` record representation changes, DWARF improvements, and a name-mangling flag were merged.
repositories.new_features:
- [#5921](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5921) (merged) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa): scannable axes now take the meet instead of overriding, with new kind abbreviations `value_maybe_null`, `value_maybe_separable`, `immutable_data_or_null`, `mutable_data_or_null`, and `sync_data_or_null`
- [#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796) (merged) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa): `float64`-only records are now represented as mixed blocks instead of float array blocks, with a legacy `[@@represent_as_float_array]` attribute escape hatch
- [#5976](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5976) (merged) by [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski): flipped `DW_AT_name` and `DW_AT_linkage_name` to comply with the DWARF5 spec
- [#5963](https://github.comml/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5963) (merged) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell): added a `-name-mangling-scheme` `ocamlopt` flag to override the per-compilation-unit name mangling convention
- [#5931](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5931) (merged) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn): removed legacy `unique_` and `once_` mode prefix syntax; all tests updated to use the modern `@` mode syntax
- [#5866](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5866) (merged) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn): layout-polymorphic values now print as `<lpoly>` in the toplevel, with appropriate checks throughout the debugger and toplevel printers
- [#5827](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5827) (merged) by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere): removed the `arg_is_non_zero` flag from `Cclz`/`Cctz` intrinsics, simplifying the CMM and operation types
- [#5929](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5929) (merged): efficiency improvements to `.cmx` export
repositories.activity_summary: Flambda2 inlining bugs fixed, register allocator affinity improved, arm64 binary emitter ordering fixed, and 5.4 merge housekeeping continued.
repositories.activity:
- [#5954](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5954) (merged) by [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury): fixed incorrect replay of inlining decisions during continuation specialization, a match-in-match correctness bug
- [#5917](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5917) (merged) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs): speculative inlining now counts the size of lifted constants, avoiding incorrect size estimates
- [#5844](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5844) (merged) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc): register allocator affinity extended to use phi-move information, reducing move counts by ~0.5–2.2% depending on allocator
- [#5978](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5978) (merged) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell): fixed arm64 binary emitter ordering bug that caused JIT code to re-enter compilerlibs and trigger zero-alloc exceptions
- [#5910](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5910) (merged) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell): added profiling instrumentation for `.cmx` loading in Flambda 2
- [#5940](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5940) (merged) by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4): added codegen tests for recently merged shift intrinsics
- [#5821](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5821) (merged): replaced `Not_found` with `Envaux.Module_not_found` for cleaner error handling
- [#5941](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5941) (merged): made `large_functor_size` effectively ignored by default
- Several [5.4 merge](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5953) PRs merged by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js), including test reference promotions, DWARF/llvmize reference updates, Dynlink mis-merge fix, Nix workflow update, and testsuite summarize.awk corrections
repositories.discussions_summary: Notable debates on metaprogramming unloading design, modality semantics on modules, and effect typechecking correctness.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960) opened by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell): large draft PR proposing GC-driven unloading of metaprogramming compilation units using `Code_block` heap objects, back-pointers in `.text`, and frame descriptor flags; [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) noted this resembles tree-shaking
- [#5966](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5966) opened by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn): proposes that modalities on module declarations apply to the module directly rather than being pushed inward onto individual items
- [#5880](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5880) (closed without merge): effect-syntax compilation fix for `maybe_region_layout` discussed at length; [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) agreed to merge and follow up on remaining type-soundness concerns with a dedicated fix in [#6001](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6001)
- [#5962](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5962) by [ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges): replacing `Map` with `Hashtbl` in the mode solver after a soundness issue with `compare_morph` was identified; active review from [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) questioning mutation safety and iteration-order stability
- [#5832](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5832) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist): arm64 frame pointer port; [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) attached a detailed Claude-generated review identifying a signal-safety regression in the prologue and nine other concerns
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing convergence toward 5.4 upstream, continued investment in metaprogramming/JIT infrastructure, and kind/layout system refinement.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **5.4 upstream merge** effort remains the most active workstream, with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) and collaborators landing a stream of small fixup PRs each day
- **Metaprogramming and JIT** infrastructure is expanding rapidly: binary emitter improvements, unloading design, and fix for constructor logic in quotations ([#5983](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5983), [#5982](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5982))
- **Kind and layout system** is being refined: scannable axes semantics changed, new `or_null` abbreviations added, `float64` record representation unified with mixed blocks — a coordinated push toward consistent unboxed-type semantics
- **DWARF debugging quality** continues to improve with name-attribute corrections, a new name-mangling flag, and an in-progress PR to attach inlined function parameters to the correct DWARF DIEs ([#5961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5961))
- **Preemptible fibers** work ([#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913)) is receiving active review as the follow-on to the initial preemption implementation

repositories.brief_summary: Improved CMI cache invalidation correctness and reduced typeopt diff vs upstream compiler.
repositories.new_features_summary: CMI cache now tracks whether hidden modules were allowed, fixing stale cache entries on visibility changes.
repositories.new_features:
- **CMI cache invalidation** improved by backporting [ocaml/merlin#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062): the `Missing` cache entry now records whether hidden modules were allowed (`hidden_were_allowed`), so a lookup with different `allow_hidden` settings correctly re-queries the filesystem instead of returning a stale negative result ([#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237))
- **`typeopt.ml` / `typeopt.mli` diff reduction** against the upstream compiler: irrelevant code sections are now commented out (rather than deleted), and error handling is improved to raise proper errors instead of `Misc.fatal_error`, making future upstream syncs easier ([#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233))
repositories.activity_summary: Two upstream-sync PRs merged; Nix flake update to OCaml 5.4 opened and awaiting merge.
repositories.activity:
- **Nix flake update** to OCaml 5.4 opened by [Gavin Gray](https://github.com/gavinleroy): the build toolchain was bumped from 5.2 to 5.4 and NixPkgs pinned to 2026-02-14 to avoid a `menhirGLR` packaging conflict ([#236](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/236)), reviewed and approved by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) with a request to add a revision comment — merged on 2026-05-04
- **CMI cache invalidation** backport merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), cherry-picking three commits from upstream and adding a new `cmi-appears` test case to verify correct cache behaviour when a `.cmi` file becomes available after a failed lookup ([#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237))
- **`typeopt` diff reduction** merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), reviewed with detailed suggestions by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) covering stub function naming and flat float array handling to further shrink the compiler delta ([#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233))
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to minimise divergence between Merlin and the upstream OCaml compiler's typing modules.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Upstream diff minimisation** continues to be a recurring focus: [#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233) is one of several PRs that systematically reduce the delta between Merlin's internal copies of compiler files (`typeopt.ml`, `typeopt.mli`) and the upstream `ocaml_flambda` branch, lowering the cost of future syncs
- **Backporting upstream fixes** to oxcaml/merlin (rather than reinventing solutions) is the preferred approach, as seen with the CMI cache fix in [#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237) which cherry-picks commits directly from [ocaml/merlin#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062)
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson authored two merged PRs; Gavin Gray opened the Nix flake update; David Allsopp and Aspen Smith provided reviews.
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) authored and merged both internal PRs this week: the `typeopt` diff reduction ([#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233)) and the CMI cache invalidation backport ([#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237)); also incorporated reviewer suggestions before merging [#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233) without a second review cycle after confirming patdiff showed no regressions
- [Gavin Gray](https://github.com/gavinleroy) opened the Nix flake update to OCaml 5.4 ([#236](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/236)) and incorporated [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark)'s review comment to document the NixPkgs pin revision
- [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) reviewed [#236](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/236), approving with a nit requesting a clarifying comment on the NixPkgs revision pin
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) reviewed [#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233) with concrete code suggestions to further reduce the `typeopt` diff, including stub function patterns and a flat float array handling approach

repositories.brief_summary: Three new PRs opened for opam-core compatibility and package additions; four older issues closed.

- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) has an ongoing draft PR [#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39) to add **`dune 3.22.2+ox`** (opened 2026-04-23, still in draft)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) closed [#15](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/15) (`opam update` error reported by [Henry Till](https://github.com/henrytill)), noting the root cause matches [ocaml/opam-repository#25961](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/25961) and was fixed in opam 2.4's switch away from an external patch implementation
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) closed [#28](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/28) (checksum failure after `oxcaml_effect` rename to `handled_effect`, reported by [Venkat Rao](https://github.com/venkatrao1)), confirming the two newer versions in the repository (`v0.18~preview.130.91+190` and `v0.18~preview.130.83+317`) carry correct checksums
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on publishing third-party packages that depend on oxcaml clarified the dependency mechanism.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35) (opened by [Nirnay Roy](https://github.com/nirnayroy)) asks how to **publish an opam package that depends on oxcaml**; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) responded that this is now supported — packages can declare `"oxcaml"` in their `depends` field following changes in commit [231c88c](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/231c88c2e564fdca40e15e750aacad5fb0887435), with policy around releasing such packages to the main opam-repository still to be determined
repositories.trends_summary: Incremental work to broaden OxCaml ecosystem coverage: new compiler-compatible package versions and ecosystem tooling.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Steady addition of **OxCaml-specific package versions** for ecosystem tooling (dune, opam-format, opam-core) reflecting upstream releases being patched for the OxCaml compiler ([#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39), [#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41), [#42](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/42))
- Growing interest in **third-party packages depending on oxcaml**, with community members beginning to explore publishing workflows ([#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35))
- Ongoing **compatibility management** between `oxcaml-compiler` minor versions and patched packages, evidenced by conflict-bound updates in [#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41)
repositories.contributors_summary: David Allsopp triaged issues and opened a new package PR; Jérôme Vouillon and art-w submitted compatibility and package PRs.
repositories.contributors:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) opened [#40](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/40) adding the `oxcaml.action-required` package, closed issues [#15](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/15) and [#28](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/28) with detailed explanations, and provided guidance on third-party publishing in [#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35)
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened [#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41) with patch updates to opam-core for `oxcaml-compiler 5.2.0minus31` compatibility
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) opened [#42](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/42) for `opam-format 2.5.1+ox` and continued work on the draft dune package PR [#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39)
