group_summaries.brief_summary: 23 packages merged in opam-repository including the oxcaml virtual package; ocaml.org received tutorial fixes and 14 new Planet posts.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New packages: oxcaml virtual, lwd 0.5, Miou ecosystem libraries, Wake-on-LAN, merlin 5.7.1-504; Hash Tables tutorial accuracy fix on ocaml.org.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **oxcaml virtual package** added to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27), providing a signalling mechanism for OxCaml dependency with a user-friendly error message guiding users to the OxCaml opam remote ([ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809))
- **lwd 0.5** released as 7 packages by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos): includes `quick_sample`/`quick_release` API additions, nested sampling detection, fixed mutation handling in Join nodes, updated `brr-lwd` for multiple reactive attributes/events, and migrated `nottui` to notty-community ([ocaml/opam-repository#29832](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29832))
- **notty-miou 0.0.1** and **nottui-miou 0.0.1** — first release of terminal library bindings for the Miou scheduler by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29833](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29833))
- **cattery 0.0.1** — first release of a pool library for the Miou scheduler by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29810](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29810))
- **wol 1.0**, **wol-eio 1.0**, and **wol-mirage 1.0** — new Wake-on-LAN magic packet construction packages with Eio and MirageOS variants by [Ryan Gibb](https://github.com/RyanGibb) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29831](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29831))
- **merlin 5.7.1-504** — fixes a cache invalidation issue introduced by hidden deps in OCaml 5.2, and improves merged index format for smaller index files with cross-references by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29828](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29828))
- **hdr_histogram 0.0.5** — ~1.8x faster value recording, new `record_value` using `int` instead of `int64`, new `hdr_percentiles_print` binding, and Windows support by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29817](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29817))
- **ppx_deriving_jsonschema 0.0.7** — adds `@@jsonschema.compact_variants`, `@@jsonschema.format`, `@@jsonschema.description`, `~ocaml_doc` flag, `@jsonschema.attrs` composite attribute, `@jsonschema.default` for record fields, makes option types nullable, and fixes schema generation for parametric recursive types ([ocaml/opam-repository#29819](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29819))
- **ciao_lwt 0.2** — compatibility with OCaml 5.4 and 5.5, plus improved handling of bound expressions in Lwt migration by [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29811](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29811))
- **charset 0.3.0** — new release by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29839](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29839))
- **Hash Tables tutorial accuracy fix** on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org): corrected O(1) complexity description from "linear" to "constant" time by [@LordPatate](https://github.com/LordPatate) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3630))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) saw 23 merges covering new packages, compatibility fixes, and infrastructure improvements; active areas included the Miou scheduler ecosystem, OxCaml integration, OCaml 5.5 compatibility, and Windows platform support
- [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) had lower volume with a merged tutorial accuracy fix ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3630)), five batches of automated OCaml Planet scraping ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3628](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3628), [#3629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3629), [#3632](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3632), [#3634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3634), [#3635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3635)), and two new community PRs under review
- Combined contributor count: approximately 27 contributors across both repositories
- Notable infrastructure milestone: the oxcaml virtual package ([ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)) establishes a pattern for how OxCaml-dependent packages signal requirements in the main opam repository
group_summaries.activity_summary: Networking, async, and compatibility packages merged in opam-repository; community event listing and Hashtbl/Map tutorial expansion opened on ocaml.org.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Networking stack packages** merged in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository): arc 0.0.2 with unverified chain signing support by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29826](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29826)); dmarc 0.0.2 updating `public_suffix_list.dat` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29808](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29808)); utcp 0.0.3 fixing TCP sequence number arithmetic per RFC 1982 by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29829](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29829))
- **Messaging and async**: nats-client 0.0.10 with async/sync dependency alignment ([ocaml/opam-repository#29816](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29816)); valkey 0.3.1 by [Avi Fenesh](https://github.com/avifenesh) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29837](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29837))
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility fixes**: caqti-miou 2.3.1 avoids exhaustiveness check failure by [Petter A. Urkedal](https://github.com/paurkedal) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29814](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29814)); upper bound added to core.v0.17.1 for `Gc.stat` record changes by [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29820](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29820)); cohttp-async 6.1.0 and 6.2.1 marked compatible with OCaml >= 5.3 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29824](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29824))
- **Proof assistant and formal methods tooling**: anders 5.0.0 and 5.1.0 with OCaml 5.0 support and new type theory primitives by [@5HT](https://github.com/5HT) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29821](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29821), [#29835](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29835)); lem 2026-05-01 release by [Brian Campbell](https://github.com/bacam) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29836](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29836)); smtml 0.26.0 with bitvector optimizations by [filipeom](https://github.com/filipeom) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29812](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29812))
- **Lwt ecosystem**: lwt 6.1.2 packaged by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29752](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29752))
- **Windows platform improvements**: conf-mingw-w64-* packages cleaned up and mechanised by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29795](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29795)); conf-perl Cygwin/MSYS2 support under review by [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29613](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29613))
- **Newly opened PRs in opam-repository**: dune 3.23.0~alpha2 with sandboxed rules by default and opam file manual promotion by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29824](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29824)); rocq-stdlib 9.1.0 draft moving package back to main opam-repository by [Matthieu Sozeau](https://github.com/mattam82) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29830](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29830)); BER MetaOCaml `avoid-version` flag draft by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29815))
- **New PR on ocaml.org**: [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho) proposed adding the recurring **Caml in the Capital** event to community listings and correcting the S-REPLS location ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3633](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3633))
- **Documentation expansion on ocaml.org**: [@LordPatate](https://github.com/LordPatate) opened a follow-up PR expanding the **Hashtbl vs Map tutorial** to explain trade-offs between the two data structures ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3631](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3631))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **OxCaml** features in both repositories: the oxcaml virtual package was merged into [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)), while [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org)'s OCaml Planet simultaneously received scraped posts covering OxCaml topics ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3632](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3632))
- **OCaml Planet on ocaml.org** aggregated community content that directly references packages and releases visible in opam-repository this week, including posts on OCaml CI, QCheck generators, and Odoc notebooks — creating a content feedback loop between the two repositories ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3628](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3628), [#3629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3629), [#3634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3634), [#3635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3635))
- **Calascibetta Romain** ([Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure)) is a contributor to both repositories: multiple packages merged in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29810](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29810), [#29833](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29833), [#29826](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29826), [#29808](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29808)) while their blog feed appears among the OCaml Planet sources tracked on ocaml.org
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- The **oxcaml virtual package** design ([ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)) prompted discussion about user-friendly error messages vs `available: false`; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) supported the action-required approach with purposeful version string ordering (`action-required < archived < latest`) as preferable to opaque solver failures
- The **lwd 0.5** release ([ocaml/opam-repository#29832](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29832)) required two review rounds to fix dependency constraint issues: `tyxml-lwd` needed a `tyxml >= 4.6.0` lower bound and `lwd` needed a `qcheck >= 0.14` bound, both fixed upstream before merging
- The **BER MetaOCaml avoid-version** draft ([ocaml/opam-repository#29815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29815)) is investigating preventing OCaml variant compilers from being selected by the solver for unrelated switches; [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) identified a `flags` vs `tags` field naming issue
- **dune 3.23.0~alpha2** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29824](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29824)) was preceded by two cancelled alpha releases (alpha0 [ocaml/opam-repository#29822](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29822) and alpha1 [ocaml/opam-repository#29823](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29823)) due to release process errors by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
- [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) reviewed the **Hashtbl vs Map documentation expansion** on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3631](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3631)), providing guidance on capitalization conventions (standard library module names should be capitalized; generic data structure terms should not) and phrasing improvements
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- The **Miou scheduler ecosystem** is expanding with three new packages this week: cattery 0.0.1 (pool), notty-miou 0.0.1 (terminals), nottui-miou 0.0.1 (UI) — all from [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) and the robur-coop community ([ocaml/opam-repository#29810](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29810), [#29833](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29833))
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** is an active concern across opam-repository: caqti-miou ([ocaml/opam-repository#29814](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29814)) and core.v0.17.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29820](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29820)) both required bounds or fixes this week due to `Gc.stat` record changes
- **OxCaml integration** into the main opam-repository infrastructure is beginning: the virtual package PR ([ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)) establishes the pattern for OxCaml-dependent packages to signal their requirements without causing opaque solver failures
- **Windows platform support** continues to expand: conf-mingw-w64-* packages mechanised ([ocaml/opam-repository#29795](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29795)), conf-perl Cygwin/MSYS2 support under review ([ocaml/opam-repository#29613](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29613)), and hdr_histogram 0.0.5 adding Windows support ([ocaml/opam-repository#29817](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29817))
- **Dune 3.23.0 alpha** preparation ([ocaml/opam-repository#29824](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29824)) signals a significant upcoming release with breaking changes: user rules sandboxed by default, removal of `--makefile` flag, and mandatory manual promotion of opam files
- **OCaml Planet feed maintenance** on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) may be needed: persistent scraping errors across multiple sources (ocamlpro, mirage, 0branch, dinosaure, emilpriver, patricoferris) and 404s from YouTube feeds appear across every scrape PR this week ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3628](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3628), [#3629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3629), [#3632](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3632), [#3634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3634), [#3635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3635))
- **Community tutorial contributions** on ocaml.org are increasing: [@LordPatate](https://github.com/LordPatate) submitted two PRs in one week targeting Hash Tables documentation — a factual fix ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3630)) followed by a broader conceptual improvement ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3631](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3631))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Three releases landed (dune 3.23.0 alpha, odoc 3.2.0, Merlin 5.7.1) alongside OxCaml compat and OCaml 5.5 Wasm work.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: dune gains %{pkg:lib:file} pforms and ppx_optcomp bootstrap; odoc 3.2.0 ships OxCaml compat; js_of_ocaml completes OCaml 5.5 Wasm.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: New `%{pkg:foo:lib:file}` pforms for querying package install layouts landed in [ocaml/dune#14200](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14200) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), closing [ocaml/dune#14193](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14193) and [ocaml/dune#3378](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3378); a draft [ocaml/dune#14373](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14373) extends this to full package sets.
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: `ppx_optcomp` conditional support in the bootstrap build added by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) in [ocaml/dune#14404](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14404), with follow-on PPX lexer clean-ups ([ocaml/dune#14406](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14406), [ocaml/dune#14408](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14408)).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: `%test_module` blocks are now stripped during bootstrap to prevent spurious build failures ([ocaml/dune#14355](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14355) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Source-files-changed event added to dune's watch-mode RPC stream by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([ocaml/dune#14396](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14396)), enabling clients to react to file-system changes without polling.
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Trace output now includes self rusage snapshots (CPU, memory, I/O counters) at build start/end via [ocaml/dune#14402](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14402) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: `Compilation_context.t` is now built per `Compilation_mode.t` — the large architectural refactor landed in [ocaml/dune#13346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13346) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), followed by [ocaml/dune#14410](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14410) and [ocaml/dune#14411](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14411).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: dune-rpc connection error handling hardened: write-side disconnects treated consistently ([ocaml/dune#14384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14384)), pending requests terminated on disconnect ([ocaml/dune#14368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14368)), write errors now raise `Connection_dead` ([ocaml/dune#14361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14361)).
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)**: Fixed typer cache invalidation bug where new `.cmi` files appearing in the build path did not trigger invalidation, causing stale type information ([ocaml/merlin#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062)); released as **Merlin v5.7.1-504**.
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)**: Refactored the `Misc` module to `ocaml_utils` to reduce the diff against the upstream compiler ([ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)).
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)**: **odoc 3.2.0 released** ([ocaml/odoc#1425](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1425)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam), including persistent LaTeX macros, multi-file `markdown-generate`, OxCaml support, OCaml 5.5 support, and bug fixes.
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)**: OxCaml compiler-libs compatibility merged by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), handling new AST nodes gracefully ([ocaml/odoc#1423](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1423)).
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)**: `@@zero_alloc` annotation support for OxCaml signatures in progress by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)).
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)**: Fixed `Not_found` crash for OCaml 5.5's `Arrow_functor` type expressions ([ocaml/odoc#1427](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1427), closes [ocaml/odoc#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426)).
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: New `OpamFilename.Unix` module added for path manipulation that always uses `/` as separator regardless of OS ([ocaml/opam#6914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6914)).
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: Fixed `OpamFile` dummy file comparison to detect dummy filenames with directory prefixes ([ocaml/opam#6913](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6913)).
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Wasm toplevel now embeds CMIs in `.wasmo` zip output, completing OCaml 5.5 Wasm separate-compilation support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2220](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2220)).
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Fixed compiler peephole rewrite for string concatenation — `+` removed from commutative operators, fixing reversed operands bug that caused `Filename.temp_file` to return malformed paths ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2228](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2228)).
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Fixed `caml_oo_cache_id` missing `return` statement in JS runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2224](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2224)) and `Int64.of_string` out-of-bounds trap in Wasm runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2223](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2223)).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Three releases across the group: dune 3.23.0 alpha0/1/2 ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)), odoc 3.2.0 ([ocaml/odoc#1425](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1425)), and Merlin v5.7.1-504 ([ocaml/merlin#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062)).
- OxCaml compatibility was a cross-cutting theme: odoc merged AST compatibility ([ocaml/odoc#1423](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1423)) and js_of_ocaml fixed its OxCaml CI workflow ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2219](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2219)) and opened an OxCaml 5.2.0minus31 update draft ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225)).
- OCaml 5.5 support landed in multiple repositories: js_of_ocaml completed Wasm support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2220](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2220)), odoc shipped it in 3.2.0 and fixed an `Arrow_functor` crash ([ocaml/odoc#1427](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1427)), and dune received a trunk build fix ([ocaml/dune#14319](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14319)).
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) was the most active repository with 30+ PRs merged or opened, driven primarily by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), and [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb).
- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) saw seven new PRs opened covering patch handling, path API modules, performance benchmarking, and documentation improvements, with two first-time contributors.
- [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format) and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) had no recorded activity this week.
group_summaries.activity_summary: Active dune 3.23.0 alpha cycle; opam patch system and path API overhauled; QuickJS-NG support and OCaml 5.6 work started in js_of_ocaml.
group_summaries.activity:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Three 3.23.0 alpha pre-releases published to opam-repository by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), tracked in [ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211); release PR [ocaml/dune#14212](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14212) remains open.
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: RPC subsystem refactoring merged across multiple PRs: server registry moved into its own module ([ocaml/dune#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14367)), Dune_engine.Rpc moved to Rpc.Global ([ocaml/dune#14362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14362)), read/write paths simplified ([ocaml/dune#14366](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14366)) — all by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Extensive observational test baselines added by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) across 14+ PRs ([ocaml/dune#14308](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14308), [ocaml/dune#14309](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14309), [ocaml/dune#14310](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14310), [ocaml/dune#14374](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14374), [ocaml/dune#14375](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14375), and others) in preparation for the per-module `-I` flag filtering work ([ocaml/dune#14186](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14186)).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Newly opened feature drafts: allocation profiling ([ocaml/dune#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401)), action runners revival ([ocaml/dune#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240)), `include_qualified` directory renaming ([ocaml/dune#13691](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13691)), and relocatable compiler integration ([ocaml/dune#14357](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14357)).
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: `ocamldep` refactoring by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) eliminated named targets ([ocaml/dune#14385](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14385)) and simplified sandbox handling ([ocaml/dune#14386](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14386)).
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: Patch system overhaul PR [ocaml/opam#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) (1777 additions) to allow `dir -> file` type changes when applying patches.
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: Path element name modules PR [ocaml/opam#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) (2072 additions across 41 files) adds explicit modules for well-known opam path component names.
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: `opam install` benchmark infrastructure added in [ocaml/opam#6912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6912) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) to enable data-driven performance decisions.
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: Two first-time contributors opened documentation PRs: [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) on manpage highlighting ([ocaml/opam#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921)) and [Corentin Coulombel](https://github.com/ccoulombel) on depopts filter documentation ([ocaml/opam#6920](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6920)).
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: QuickJS-NG support proposed by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229): adds a `quickjs` dune profile, `quickjs_shim.js` compatibility shim, host-portability bug fixes, and a `make tests-quickjs` CI target.
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Initial OCaml 5.6 support draft opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227); OCaml 5.5 support tracking issue [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2193](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2193) closed.
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: Large DOM API type corrections opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221) (3215 additions), fixing `inputElement.defaultChecked`, `createVideo` return type, and `selection.collapse` signature.
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)**: Dependabot opened [ocaml/merlin#2061](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2061) to bump `cachix/install-nix-action` (includes a Nix sandbox privilege escalation security fix).
- **[ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind)**: [@shym](https://github.com/shym) opened CI workflow update PR [ocaml/ocamlfind#116](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/116) and rebased the threads exception fix [ocaml/ocamlfind#99](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/99) for systhreads-disabled builds.
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)**: Automated flake.lock update ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)) refreshed `merlin5_3` and `nixpkgs` dependencies.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **OxCaml compatibility** spans three repositories: odoc merged compiler-libs AST compatibility ([ocaml/odoc#1423](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1423)), js_of_ocaml fixed its OxCaml CI workflow ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2219](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2219)) and opened an OxCaml 5.2.0minus31 update ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225)), and dune's OxCaml trunk build was fixed by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) ([ocaml/dune#14319](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14319)).
- **dune + odoc integration**: [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) added a test in dune reproducing the `dune build @doc` failure when odoc is installed via `dune tools install` ([ocaml/dune#14234](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14234)), directly connecting the two projects.
- **[Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) active in both dune and odoc**: managed the dune 3.23.0 alpha release cycle ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) and filed the original `@@zero_alloc` support issue in odoc ([ocaml/odoc#1418](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1418)) that [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) is now implementing ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)).
- **[Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) active in both opam and js_of_ocaml**: driving opam performance benchmarking ([ocaml/opam#6912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6912)) and API design issues ([ocaml/opam#6918](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6918), [ocaml/opam#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919)), while commenting on OCaml 5.5 release timing in js_of_ocaml ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2193](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2193)).
- **ocaml-lsp flake.lock includes Merlin**: the automated [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) update bumped `merlin5_3` to commit `03c8e3f` (2026-04-30), tracking the ocaml/merlin release.
- **ocamlfind systhreads fix references MirageOS**: the threads exception fix [ocaml/ocamlfind#99](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/99) traces back to a failure reported in [mirage/ocaml-solo5#148](https://github.com/mirage/ocaml-solo5/issues/148), connecting the fix to unikernel toolchain concerns.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **`DUNE_PKG` environment variable** proposal ([ocaml/dune#14318](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14318) by [@desumn](https://github.com/desumn)) was discussed at the dune dev-meeting and closed; consensus was insufficient user demand to add an environment variable for overriding the dune package variable.
- **ocamlgrep integration into Merlin** proposed by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) in [ocaml/merlin#2063](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2063): the tool enables project-wide type-aware pattern search (e.g. `(__ : int list)` to find all expressions of type `int list`). [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) confirmed full LexiFi support for the proposal; [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) asked whether Sherlodoc already covers similar ground.
- **Merlin typer cache root cause traced**: during review of [ocaml/merlin#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062), [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) connected the cache bug to a two-year-old prediction by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) in [ocaml/ocaml#12246](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12246).
- **`--assume-built` with `ocaml-variants` failure**: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) reported in [ocaml/opam#6911](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6911) that the new `make INSTALL_MODE=opam install` instruction creates `ocaml-compiler-fixup.sh` instead of `ocaml-variants-fixup.sh`; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) identified both upstream OCaml compiler docs and `ocaml-variants.opam` need updating.
- **Neutral JS target environment** requested by [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226) for runtimes like QuickJS, noting that `--target-env=browser` is counter-intuitive for QuickJS targets; this connects to [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)'s QuickJS-NG PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)).
- **CPS tail-call recursion depth issue** identified by [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215): `"Uncaught InternalError: too much recursion"` after Babel transpilation of CPS-style functions, highlighting difficulties debugging transpiled OCaml on constrained JS engines.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml compatibility as a sustained group-wide effort**: three repositories (ocaml/dune, ocaml/odoc, ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) simultaneously addressing OxCaml compiler-libs changes, suggesting coordinated upstream tracking rather than ad-hoc fixes.
- **Per-compilation-mode context separation in dune**: the architectural direction of [ocaml/dune#13346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13346) (landed this week) separating native/Melange/other modes into distinct `Compilation_context.t` instances, with follow-ons [ocaml/dune#14411](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14411) and [ocaml/dune#14410](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14410) extending it; unlocks per-mode object directories and cleaner mode-specific rules.
- **RPC subsystem hardening in dune**: multiple disconnect and error handling fixes ([ocaml/dune#14361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14361), [ocaml/dune#14368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14368), [ocaml/dune#14384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14384)) combined with structural refactors ([ocaml/dune#14362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14362), [ocaml/dune#14366](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14366), [ocaml/dune#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14367)) suggest the RPC layer is being prepared for the planned action runners ([ocaml/dune#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240)).
- **Broader JS runtime support in js_of_ocaml**: QuickJS-NG PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)), the `--target-env=neutral` request ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226)), and browser compatibility documentation ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)) all push toward running js_of_ocaml output on engines beyond Node.js.
- **Reducing compiler diffs in Merlin**: moving modules like `Misc` into compiler-synced locations ([ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)) continues as a sustained effort to lower maintenance burden for OCaml version upgrades.
- **Performance measurement infrastructure in opam**: [ocaml/opam#6912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6912) benchmark infrastructure enables data-driven decisions, paired with active API surface cleanup ([ocaml/opam#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910), [ocaml/opam#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680)) and Windows path correctness improvements ([ocaml/opam#6914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6914), [ocaml/opam#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917)).
- **Test baseline practice in dune**: [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb)'s 14+ observational test PRs ([ocaml/dune#14308](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14308) through [ocaml/dune#14391](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14391)) locking in current behavior before planned per-module dependency changes indicate larger module-level dependency changes are forthcoming.

group_summaries.brief_summary: 20 commits landed in oxcaml/oxcaml spanning kind/layout, DWARF, and flambda2; merlin and opam-repository also saw updates.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New kind abbreviations, float64 record representation change, DWARF5 compliance fix, name-mangling flag, and CMI cache fix merged.
group_summaries.new_features:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5921](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5921) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa): scannable axes now take the meet instead of overriding; new kind abbreviations `value_maybe_null`, `value_maybe_separable`, `immutable_data_or_null`, `mutable_data_or_null`, and `sync_data_or_null` added
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa): `float64`-only records are now represented as mixed blocks instead of float array blocks, with a legacy `[@@represent_as_float_array]` attribute for compatibility
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5976](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5976) by [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski): flipped `DW_AT_name` and `DW_AT_linkage_name` to comply with the DWARF5 spec
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5963](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5963) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell): added a `-name-mangling-scheme` `ocamlopt` flag to override the per-compilation-unit name mangling convention
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5931](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5931) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn): removed legacy `unique_` and `once_` mode prefix syntax; all tests updated to use the modern `@` mode syntax
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5866](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5866) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn): layout-polymorphic values now print as `<lpoly>` in the toplevel, with appropriate checks in the debugger and toplevel printers
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5827](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5827) by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere): removed the `arg_is_non_zero` flag from `Cclz`/`Cctz` intrinsics, simplifying CMM and operation types
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5929](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5929): efficiency improvements to `.cmx` export
- [oxcaml/merlin#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923): CMI cache now records `hidden_were_allowed` in `Missing` entries, fixing stale negative cache results when `allow_hidden` settings change (backport of [ocaml/merlin#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062))
- [oxcaml/merlin#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923): `typeopt.ml`/`typeopt.mli` diff against upstream compiler reduced — irrelevant sections commented out, error handling improved to raise proper errors instead of `Misc.fatal_error`
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) was the most active repository with 20 commits spanning the kind/layout system, flambda2 inlining, backend fixes, DWARF debugging, and ongoing 5.4 upstream merge work
- [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) had 3 new PRs opened for compiler-compatible package additions (opam-core patch, opam-format, dune) and 4 older issues closed
- [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) merged 2 PRs — a CMI cache correctness fix ([#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237)) and a compiler diff reduction ([#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233)) — plus a Nix flake update ([#236](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/236))
- [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml) and [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) had no recorded activity this week
- Over 15 contributors were active across the group, with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) contributing across three repositories
group_summaries.activity_summary: Flambda2 inlining bugs fixed, register allocator improved, arm64 emitter bug fixed, and opam ecosystem packages updated for oxcaml-compiler compatibility.
group_summaries.activity:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5954](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5954) by [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury): fixed incorrect replay of inlining decisions during continuation specialization (match-in-match correctness bug)
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5917](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5917) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs): speculative inlining now accounts for the size of lifted constants, correcting previously incorrect size estimates
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5844](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5844) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc): register allocator affinity extended to use phi-move information, reducing move counts by ~0.5–2.2% depending on allocator
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5978](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5978) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell): fixed arm64 binary emitter ordering bug that caused JIT code to re-enter compilerlibs and trigger zero-alloc exceptions
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5910](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5910) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell): added profiling instrumentation for `.cmx` loading in Flambda 2
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5940](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5940) by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4): added codegen tests for recently merged shift intrinsics
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5821](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5821): replaced `Not_found` with `Envaux.Module_not_found` for cleaner error handling
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5941](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5941): made `large_functor_size` effectively ignored by default
- Several 5.4 merge fixup PRs landed including [oxcaml/oxcaml#5953](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5953) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js), covering test reference promotions, DWARF/llvmize reference updates, Dynlink mis-merge fix, Nix workflow update, and testsuite corrections
- [oxcaml/opam-repository#40](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/40) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js): added `oxcaml.action-required` package (with `available: false`) mirroring [ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)
- [oxcaml/opam-repository#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon): updated opam-core OxCaml patch for `oxcaml-compiler 5.2.0minus31` compatibility, removing the upper-bound conflict
- [oxcaml/opam-repository#42](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/42) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w): added `opam-format 2.5.1+ox`, needed transitively for odoc; depends on [#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41) merging first
- [oxcaml/opam-repository#15](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/15) and [oxcaml/opam-repository#28](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/28) closed by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) with root-cause explanations
- [oxcaml/merlin#236](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/236) by [Gavin Gray](https://github.com/gavinleroy): Nix flake build toolchain bumped from OCaml 5.2 to 5.4, NixPkgs pinned to 2026-02-14 to avoid a `menhirGLR` packaging conflict
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) was active across three repositories this week: landing 5.4 merge fixups in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) ([#5953](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5953) and related), adding the `oxcaml.action-required` package in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) ([#40](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/40)), and reviewing `typeopt` diff reduction in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) ([#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233))
- The [oxcaml/opam-repository#42](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/42) `opam-format 2.5.1+ox` PR has an explicit blocking dependency on [oxcaml/opam-repository#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41) (opam-core patch) merging first, due to the `extlib` dependency chain
- The `dune 3.22.2+ox` draft PR [oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39) reflects ongoing work to keep build tooling current with [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) compiler releases
- [oxcaml/merlin#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237) cherry-picks commits directly from [ocaml/merlin#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062), demonstrating the pattern of backporting upstream merlin fixes into the oxcaml fork
- The 5.4 upstream merge in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) and the Nix flake OCaml 5.4 bump in [oxcaml/merlin#236](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/236) are coordinated: both reflect the group's progression toward the OCaml 5.4 release baseline
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960) opened by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell): large draft PR proposing GC-driven unloading of metaprogramming compilation units using `Code_block` heap objects, back-pointers in `.text`, and frame descriptor flags; [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) noted the resemblance to tree-shaking
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5966](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5966) opened by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn): proposes that modalities on module declarations apply to the module directly rather than being pushed inward onto individual items
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5880](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5880): effect-syntax compilation fix for `maybe_region_layout` discussed at length; [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) agreed to merge and track remaining type-soundness concerns in a dedicated follow-up [oxcaml/oxcaml#6001](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6001)
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5962](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5962) by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges): replacing `Map` with `Hashtbl` in the mode solver after a soundness issue with `compare_morph` was identified; [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) raised questions about mutation safety and iteration-order stability
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5832](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5832) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist): arm64 frame pointer port; [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) provided a detailed review identifying a signal-safety regression in the prologue and nine other concerns
- [oxcaml/opam-repository#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35) by [Nirnay Roy](https://github.com/nirnayroy): asked how to publish an opam package depending on oxcaml; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) confirmed this is now supported via the `"oxcaml"` `depends` field (since commit [231c88c](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/231c88c2e564fdca40e15e750aacad5fb0887435)), with policy for releasing such packages to the main opam-repository still to be determined
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- The **5.4 upstream merge** remains the highest-activity workstream in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) and collaborators landing a stream of fixup PRs (e.g. [#5953](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5953)); the [oxcaml/merlin#236](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/236) Nix flake bump to OCaml 5.4 tracks the same progression
- **Metaprogramming and JIT** infrastructure is expanding: binary emitter correctness ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5978](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5978)), GC-driven unloading design ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960)), and quotation constructor fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5983](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5983), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5982](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5982)) are active in parallel
- **Kind and layout system** refinement continues with coordinated changes: scannable axes semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5921](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5921)), `float64` record unboxing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796)), and new `or_null` abbreviations — a coherent push toward consistent unboxed-type semantics
- **DWARF debugging quality** is receiving sustained attention: DWARF5 name attribute compliance ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5976](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5976)), name-mangling flag ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5963](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5963)), and an in-progress PR for inlined function parameter attribution ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5961))
- **Preemptible fibers** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913)) are receiving active review as the follow-on to the initial preemption implementation
- **Upstream diff minimisation** in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) is a recurring theme: [#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233) reduces the `typeopt.ml` delta and [#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237) backports an upstream fix, reflecting a preference for cherry-picking over local reinvention
- **OxCaml ecosystem package coverage** is growing incrementally, with PRs for dune ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39)), opam-core ([oxcaml/opam-repository#41](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/41)), and opam-format ([oxcaml/opam-repository#42](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/42)) all active, alongside growing community interest in third-party package publishing ([oxcaml/opam-repository#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Seq constant-space improvements merged, OCamlFDO compatibility restored, and the modular IO RFC revived via a new ocaml/ocaml PR.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Seq gained constant-space cycle/repeat/forever; Value_rec_check switched to Ident.Map; OCamlFDO symbol names restored.
group_summaries.new_features:
- [Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) reduced closure allocation in `Seq.(cycle, repeat, forever)` by reusing tail closures in [ocaml/ocaml#14781](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14781), merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) improved `Value_rec_check` in [ocaml/ocaml#14777](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14777) by switching to `Ident.Map` and using `Map.union` for join, merged by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) restored hard-wired `caml_hot__code_begin`/`caml_hot__code_end` symbol names required by OCamlFDO and moved hot-code segment registration into `startup_nat.c` in [ocaml/ocaml#14778](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14778), merged by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) merged six or more PRs this week spanning Stdlib `Seq` performance, man page improvements, OCamlFDO compatibility, typechecker debuggability, and CI security hardening.
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) saw a single event: [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) withdrew his 2022 proposal [ocaml/RFCs#30](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/30) to remove the modular IO RFC, citing lack of intent to implement it himself.
- The modular IO topic connects both repositories: the RFC withdrawal in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) coincides with [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) opening [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) proposing extensible IO channels implementing that same RFC in the compiler repo.
- Ten contributors were active across the group, with merged work from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes), [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), [Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf), [@shym](https://github.com/shym), [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128), and [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Man page overhaul, typechecker debugger additions, CI security fix, and Seq follow-up PRs dominated open and merged activity in ocaml/ocaml.
group_summaries.activity:
- [@shym](https://github.com/shym) overhauled the `ocamlc` man page in [ocaml/ocaml#14782](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14782) with improved formatting, synopsis reformatting, sub-list rendering, and `-o` option clarifications, plus minor fixes to the `ocamlopt` man page; merged 2026-05-03.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) added a breakpoint capability on `tfunctor` nodes in the typechecker debugger in [ocaml/ocaml#14771](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14771) and improved unscoped-identifier printing in [ocaml/ocaml#14772](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14772); both merged 2026-04-27.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) documented a newly found typechecker loop triggered by conjunctive types after `Keep_expansion` in [ocaml/ocaml#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769); merged 2026-04-29.
- [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128) fixed shell injection vulnerabilities in GitHub Actions workflows in [ocaml/ocaml#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676); merged 2026-05-04.
- [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783) making `Seq.repeat` constant-space via a single cyclic closure-and-cons-cell pair (merged post-week) and [ocaml/ocaml#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784) proposing a fully cyclic `Seq.cycle` reducing per-element memory from 41 words to 1 word; the latter remains open under discussion.
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14779) proposing a fix for the typechecker loop in [ocaml/ocaml#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769) by detecting cycles in `update_level`; open and under review.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14776](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14776) fixing a missing `wrap_trace_gadts_instance` call in `moregen` when checking first-class module inclusion; open and under review.
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14786](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14786) to update GitHub Actions dependencies and avoid outdated Node version warnings; open and awaiting review.
- [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) closed [ocaml/RFCs#30](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/30) (open since 2022-02-03) without merging, noting he no longer plans to implement the proposal and inviting others to re-open or write an updated version.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work: - [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) withdrew [ocaml/RFCs#30](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/30) (remove the modular IO RFC) while simultaneously opening [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) proposing extensible IO channels that implement the very RFC he chose not to remove — indicating renewed momentum around that design in the compiler rather than at the RFC level.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784) and [ocaml/ocaml#14785](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14785) sparked a debate between [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) and [Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) on whether `Seq.cycle` should produce a truly cyclic (sharing) structure or a constant-space infinite one; [ocaml/ocaml#14785](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14785) was closed as superseded by [ocaml/ocaml#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14780](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14780) opened by [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) reports that `module type of M` when `M` is accessed through an alias unexpectedly strengthens functor parameter signatures, blocking application to sibling modules; attracted discussion on the intended semantics of module alias strengthening.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14779) discusses the root cause of the typechecker loop in [ocaml/ocaml#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769): `update_level` encountering the expanded type of its own argument, and the correct approach to detect and break that cycle.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Seq constant-space work** is converging across multiple PRs: [ocaml/ocaml#14781](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14781) landed, [ocaml/ocaml#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783) merged post-week, and [ocaml/ocaml#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784) proposes a fully cyclic `Seq.cycle`, showing sustained effort from [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) and [Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) to minimise allocation in lazy sequences.
- **Typechecker debuggability** additions by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml#14771](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14771), [ocaml/ocaml#14772](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14772)) continue a pattern of building internal tooling to support OCaml 5.5 typechecker development.
- **Native-code tooling compatibility** work in [ocaml/ocaml#14778](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14778) reflects continued effort to keep the runtime compatible with profile-guided optimisation workflows such as OCamlFDO.
- **Modular IO** is regaining attention: the RFC withdrawal in [ocaml/RFCs#30](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/30) and the new implementation proposal [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) together suggest the design may advance at the implementation level rather than through further RFC revision.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Minimal activity across the multicore group; ocaml-uring closed a stale draft PR addressing Linux 5.x kernel header compatibility.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was limited to a single repository: [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring).
- The remaining 8 repositories ([eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio), [domainslib](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib), [meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio), [saturn](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/saturn), [eio-trace](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio-trace), [picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos), [kcas](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/kcas), [ocaml-iomux](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux)) recorded no activity during this period.
group_summaries.activity_summary: A long-standing draft PR in ocaml-uring addressing Linux 5.x kernel header compatibility was closed without merging.
group_summaries.activity: - [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#110](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/110): a draft PR from [Chris Armstrong](https://github.com/chris-armstrong) to import `fcntl.h` from `linux/fcntl.h` for access to io_uring constants only available on Linux 5.x kernels was closed without merging; it had accumulated merge conflicts and remained in draft state since October 2023.

weekly_summary.brief_summary: odoc 3.2.0, merlin 5.7.1-504, and dune 3.23.0 alpha released; OxCaml kind system refined; oxcaml virtual package established in opam-repository.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: odoc 3.2.0 adds OxCaml and OCaml 5.5 support; dune gains pforms and RPC events; js_of_ocaml completes Wasm 5.5; Seq gets constant-space closures.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **odoc 3.2.0 released** ([ocaml/odoc#1425](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1425)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam), shipping **OxCaml compiler-libs compatibility** ([ocaml/odoc#1423](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1423)), **persistent LaTeX macros** for HTML/KaTeX ([ocaml/odoc#1391](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1391)), **multi-file `markdown-generate`** eliminating shell scripting ([ocaml/odoc#1387](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1387)), OCaml 5.5 support ([ocaml/odoc#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406)), and a fix for `Arrow_functor` `Not_found` crashes ([ocaml/odoc#1427](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1427))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin v5.7.1-504 released** ([ocaml/merlin#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062)) by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), fixing a **CMI cache invalidation bug** where new `.cmi` files in the build path failed to trigger invalidation due to hidden deps in OCaml 5.2; merged indexes now point to each other, producing smaller index files
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune 3.23.0 alpha0/1/2 released** ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) with new `%{pkg:foo:lib:file}` pforms for querying package install layouts ([ocaml/dune#14200](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14200)), **source-files-changed events** in the watch-mode RPC stream ([ocaml/dune#14396](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14396)), and **trace output with rusage snapshots** at build start/end ([ocaml/dune#14402](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14402))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`Compilation_context.t` per-`Compilation_mode.t`** architectural refactor landed in [ocaml/dune#13346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13346) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), separating native/Melange/other modes into distinct contexts with follow-ons ([ocaml/dune#14410](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14410), [ocaml/dune#14411](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14411))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune-rpc connection error handling hardened** across three PRs by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg): write-side disconnects treated consistently ([ocaml/dune#14384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14384)), pending requests terminated on disconnect ([ocaml/dune#14368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14368)), write errors now raise `Connection_dead` ([ocaml/dune#14361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14361))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`ppx_optcomp` conditional support** added to the dune bootstrap build by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([ocaml/dune#14404](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14404)), with follow-on PPX lexer cleanups ([ocaml/dune#14406](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14406), [ocaml/dune#14408](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14408))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`%test_module` blocks stripped during bootstrap** to prevent spurious build failures by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([ocaml/dune#14355](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14355))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`OpamFilename.Unix` module added** to opam for path manipulation using `/` as separator regardless of OS ([ocaml/opam#6914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6914))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Wasm toplevel embeds CMIs** in `.wasmo` zip output in js_of_ocaml, completing OCaml 5.5 Wasm separate-compilation support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2220](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2220))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **String concatenation peephole rewrite fixed** in js_of_ocaml — `+` removed from commutative operators, fixing reversed operands that caused `Filename.temp_file` to return malformed paths ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2228](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2228))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin `Misc` module refactored to `ocaml_utils`** to reduce diff against the upstream compiler ([ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **New kind abbreviations** landed in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5921](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5921) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa): `value_maybe_null`, `value_maybe_separable`, `immutable_data_or_null`, `mutable_data_or_null`, and `sync_data_or_null`; scannable axes now take the meet instead of overriding
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`float64`-only records now represented as mixed blocks** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796)) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), with `[@@represent_as_float_array]` attribute for legacy compatibility
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **DWARF5 `DW_AT_name`/`DW_AT_linkage_name` compliance fixed** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5976](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5976)) by [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`-name-mangling-scheme` flag added** to `ocamlopt` by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) to override per-compilation-unit name mangling convention ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5963](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5963))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Legacy `unique_` and `once_` mode prefix syntax removed** by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn); all tests updated to use the modern `@` mode syntax ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5931](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5931))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Layout-polymorphic values now print as `<lpoly>`** in the toplevel with appropriate debugger and printer checks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5866](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5866)) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`arg_is_non_zero` flag removed** from `Cclz`/`Cctz` intrinsics by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere), simplifying CMM and operation types ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5827](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5827))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Register allocator affinity extended** to use phi-move information by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc), reducing move counts by ~0.5–2.2% ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5844](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5844))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **OxCaml merlin CMI cache fix backported** — `hidden_were_allowed` now recorded in `Missing` entries to prevent stale negative cache results ([oxcaml/merlin#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237)) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Seq.(cycle, repeat, forever)` closure allocation reduced** by [Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) via tail closure reuse ([ocaml/ocaml#14781](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14781)), merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Value_rec_check` switched to `Ident.Map` with `Map.union`** join by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) ([ocaml/ocaml#14777](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14777)), merged by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **OCamlFDO symbol names restored** — `caml_hot__code_begin`/`caml_hot__code_end` hard-wired and hot-code registration moved to `startup_nat.c` by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([ocaml/ocaml#14778](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14778))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **oxcaml virtual package** added to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27), establishing the pattern for OxCaml-dependent packages to signal requirements without opaque solver failures

-**dune-rpc connection error handling hardened** across three PRs by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg): write-side disconnects treated consistently ([ocaml/dune#14384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14384)), pending requests terminated on disconnect ([ocaml/dune#14368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14368)), write errors now raise `Connection_dead` ([ocaml/dune#14361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14361))

-**`ppx_optcomp` conditional support** added to the dune bootstrap build by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([ocaml/dune#14404](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14404)), with follow-on PPX lexer cleanups ([ocaml/dune#14406](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14406), [ocaml/dune#14408](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14408))
-**`%test_module` blocks stripped during bootstrap** to prevent spurious build failures by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([ocaml/dune#14355](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14355))
-**`OpamFilename.Unix` module added** to opam for path manipulation using `/` as separator regardless of OS ([ocaml/opam#6914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6914))

-**Wasm toplevel embeds CMIs** in `.wasmo` zip output in js_of_ocaml, completing OCaml 5.5 Wasm separate-compilation support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2220](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2220))

-**String concatenation peephole rewrite fixed** in js_of_ocaml — `+` removed from commutative operators, fixing reversed operands that caused `Filename.temp_file` to return malformed paths ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2228](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2228))
-**Merlin `Misc` module refactored to `ocaml_utils`** to reduce diff against the upstream compiler ([ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059))

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Three releases shipped this week: **odoc 3.2.0** ([ocaml/odoc#1425](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1425)) with OxCaml and OCaml 5.5 support, **merlin v5.7.1-504** ([ocaml/merlin#2062](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2062)) fixing a CMI cache invalidation bug, and **dune 3.23.0~alpha0/1/2** ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) kicking off a pre-release cycle; [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) was the most active repository with 30+ PRs merged driven by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), and [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) landed 20 commits across the **kind/layout system** (new `or_null` abbreviations, `float64` mixed-block representation, legacy mode prefix removal), **DWARF debugging** (DWARF5 compliance, name-mangling flag), **flambda2** (inlining decision replay fix, speculative inlining size correction), and ongoing **5.4 upstream merge** fixups from [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) merged improvements to **Seq** constant-space allocation ([ocaml/ocaml#14781](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14781)), **typechecker debuggability** ([ocaml/ocaml#14771](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14771), [ocaml/ocaml#14772](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14772)), **OCamlFDO** compatibility ([ocaml/ocaml#14778](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14778)), and a **GitHub Actions security fix** ([ocaml/ocaml#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676)); [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) revived the modular IO topic by withdrawing [ocaml/RFCs#30](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/30) while opening implementation PR [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) processed 23 merges including the **oxcaml virtual package** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)), **lwd 0.5** (7 packages), three **Miou ecosystem** libraries, and several networking and formal-methods packages; [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) received a tutorial accuracy fix and 14 new OCaml Planet posts
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ Activity across the multicore group was minimal; [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) was the only active repository, with a long-standing draft PR ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#110](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/110)) for Linux 5.x kernel header compatibility closed without merging after accumulating merge conflicts since October 2023

weekly_summary.activity_summary: Dune RPC subsystem refactored, opam patch system overhauled, js_of_ocaml QuickJS-NG and OCaml 5.6 drafts opened, arm64 emitter bug fixed in OxCaml.
weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune RPC subsystem refactored** across multiple PRs by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg): server registry moved to its own module ([ocaml/dune#14367](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14367)), `Dune_engine.Rpc` moved to `Rpc.Global` ([ocaml/dune#14362](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14362)), and read/write paths simplified ([ocaml/dune#14366](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14366)); the refactors accompany the action runners revival draft ([ocaml/dune#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`ocamldep` refactored** in dune by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg): named targets eliminated ([ocaml/dune#14385](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14385)) and sandbox handling simplified ([ocaml/dune#14386](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14386))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Observational test baselines** added by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) across 14+ dune PRs ([ocaml/dune#14308](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14308), [ocaml/dune#14309](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14309), [ocaml/dune#14310](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14310), [ocaml/dune#14374](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14374), [ocaml/dune#14375](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14375)) in preparation for per-module `-I` flag filtering ([ocaml/dune#14186](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14186))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Opam patch system overhaul** by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) — 1777 additions in [ocaml/opam#6915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6915) to allow `dir -> file` type changes when applying patches; **path element name modules** in [ocaml/opam#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917) add explicit modules for well-known opam path component names (2072 additions across 41 files)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`opam install` benchmark infrastructure** added by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) in [ocaml/opam#6912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6912) to enable data-driven performance decisions
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Two first-time contributors opened opam documentation PRs: [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) on manpage highlighting ([ocaml/opam#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921)) and [Corentin Coulombel](https://github.com/ccoulombel) on depopts filter documentation ([ocaml/opam#6920](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6920))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **QuickJS-NG support proposed** by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229): adds a `quickjs` dune profile, `quickjs_shim.js` compatibility shim, host-portability bug fixes, and `make tests-quickjs` CI target
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Initial OCaml 5.6 support draft** opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227); OCaml 5.5 tracking issue [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2193](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2193) closed
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Large DOM API type corrections** opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221) (3215 additions), fixing `inputElement.defaultChecked`, `createVideo` return type, and `selection.collapse` signature
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`caml_oo_cache_id` missing `return` statement** fixed in the JS runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2224](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2224)) and `Int64.of_string` out-of-bounds trap fixed in Wasm runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2223](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2223))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **ocamlfind CI workflow** updated by [@shym](https://github.com/shym) in [ocaml/ocamlfind#116](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/116) and the threads exception fix [ocaml/ocamlfind#99](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/99) rebased for systhreads-disabled builds
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **arm64 binary emitter ordering bug fixed** by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5978](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5978), preventing JIT code from re-entering compilerlibs and triggering zero-alloc exceptions

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Seq.cycle` memory model debate** ([ocaml/ocaml#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784)): [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) and [Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) debated whether `Seq.cycle` should produce a truly cyclic sharing structure or a constant-space infinite one; a related PR [ocaml/ocaml#14785](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14785) was closed as superseded, while the cyclic approach reducing per-element memory from 41 words to 1 word remains under discussion
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Module alias strengthening semantics** ([ocaml/ocaml#14780](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14780)): [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) reported that `module type of M` when `M` is accessed through an alias unexpectedly strengthens functor parameter signatures, blocking application to sibling modules; attracted discussion on intended alias strengthening semantics
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Typechecker `update_level` cycle** ([ocaml/ocaml#14779](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14779)): [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) proposed a fix for the typechecker loop documented in [ocaml/ocaml#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769) by detecting cycles in `update_level`; the root cause — `update_level` encountering the expanded type of its own argument — is under active review
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **GC-driven metaprogramming unit unloading** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960)): large draft PR by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) proposing GC-driven unloading of metaprogramming compilation units using `Code_block` heap objects, back-pointers in `.text`, and frame descriptor flags; [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) noted the resemblance to tree-shaking
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Modalities on module declarations** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5966](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5966)): [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) proposes that modalities on module declarations apply to the module directly rather than being pushed inward onto individual items
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Mode solver `Map` vs `Hashtbl` soundness** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5962](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5962)): replacing `Map` with `Hashtbl` in the mode solver after a `compare_morph` soundness issue; [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) raised questions about mutation safety and iteration-order stability
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **arm64 frame pointer port review** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5832](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5832)): [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) provided a detailed review of [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)'s port identifying a signal-safety regression in the prologue and nine other concerns
