weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml compatibility as a sustained group-wide effort**: odoc 3.2.0 ([ocaml/odoc#1425](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1425)), js_of_ocaml ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2219](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2219), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225)), and dune ([ocaml/dune#14319](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14319)) all addressed OxCaml compiler-libs changes in the same week, continuing the coordinated upstream tracking pattern observed in weeks 15–17
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Kind and layout system convergence**: scannable axes semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5921](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5921)), `float64` record unboxing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796)), new `or_null` abbreviations, and legacy mode syntax removal ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5931](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5931)) together represent a coherent push toward consistent unboxed-type semantics — the `float64` mixed-block change is the third in a series (following weeks 16–17's uniform block work by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **DWARF debugging quality receiving sustained attention**: DWARF5 name attribute compliance ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5976](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5976)), name-mangling flag ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5963](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5963)), and an in-progress inlined function parameter attribution PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5961)) reflect a multi-week effort to improve native code debug output quality
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Metaprogramming and JIT infrastructure expanding in parallel**: binary emitter correctness ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5978](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5978)), GC-driven unloading design ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5960](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5960)), and quotation constructor fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5983](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5983), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5982](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5982)) are all active, suggesting the metaprogramming feature is approaching production hardening
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Per-compilation-mode context separation in dune**: the architectural refactor of `Compilation_context.t` ([ocaml/dune#13346](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13346)) with follow-ons ([ocaml/dune#14411](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14411), [ocaml/dune#14410](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14410)) unlocks per-mode object directories and cleaner mode-specific rules; combined with RPC hardening ([ocaml/dune#14361](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14361), [ocaml/dune#14368](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14368), [ocaml/dune#14384](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14384)) suggests the RPC layer is being prepared for the planned action runners ([ocaml/dune#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Broader JS runtime support in js_of_ocaml**: QuickJS-NG PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)), the `--target-env=neutral` request ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226)), and OCaml 5.6 support draft ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2227](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2227)) all signal an expansion beyond Node.js-only targets — with the OCaml 5.5 tracking issue ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2193](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2193)) now closed, 5.6 tracking is beginning immediately
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Seq constant-space work converging**: [ocaml/ocaml#14781](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14781) landed, [ocaml/ocaml#14783](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14783) merged post-week, and [ocaml/ocaml#14784](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14784) proposes a fully cyclic `Seq.cycle`, showing sustained effort from [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) and [Haz](https://github.com/hyphenrf) to minimise allocation in lazy sequences
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Modular IO revival at implementation level**: the RFC withdrawal in [ocaml/RFCs#30](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/30) and the new implementation proposal [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) together suggest the design may advance through concrete code rather than further RFC revision
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Miou scheduler ecosystem growing**: cattery 0.0.1 (pool), notty-miou 0.0.1 (terminals), and nottui-miou 0.0.1 (UI) all from [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) and the robur-coop community ([ocaml/opam-repository#29810](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29810), [ocaml/opam-repository#29833](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29833)), extending the Miou ecosystem into terminal UI and connection pooling
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OxCaml integration into main opam-repository infrastructure**: the virtual package PR ([ocaml/opam-repository#29809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29809)) and growing community interest in third-party OxCaml package publishing ([oxcaml/opam-repository#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35)) indicate OxCaml is beginning to establish a presence in the main opam ecosystem, with policy for community packages still being determined
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml Planet feed maintenance needed**: persistent scraping errors across multiple sources (ocamlpro, mirage, 0branch, dinosaure, emilpriver, patricoferris) and 404s from YouTube feeds appear across every scrape PR this week ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3628](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3628), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3629), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3632](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3632), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3634), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3635](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3635))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Upstream diff minimisation in OxCaml merlin**: [oxcaml/merlin#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233) reduces the `typeopt.ml` delta and [oxcaml/merlin#237](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/237) backports an upstream fix, while [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) moves `Misc` to `ocaml_utils` in upstream merlin — a convergent effort across both repositories to lower maintenance burden for OCaml version upgrades
